title: Warm Creamy Greek Thai Eggplant Saganaki
description: A salty, creamy, and light Greek dish featuring Thai eggplant, score fat, and satvik friendly ingredients, served in a saganaki form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the Thai eggplant into 1/2 inch thick rounds and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the score fat over the eggplant and sprinkle with salt, black pepper, and chili flakes if using.
Roast the eggplant in the oven for 20 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
While the eggplant is roasting, mix the feta cheese, garlic, and lemon juice in a bowl.
After the eggplant has roasted, remove it from the oven and top each round with a spoonful of the feta mixture.
Return the eggplant to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the eggplant from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Serve the Warm Creamy Greek Thai Eggplant Saganaki hot, with a side of Greek yogurt for dipping.
